author: @stevetobias4890
text: There is another way to finish off, make your own dowels with a dark wood, even better, cut along the corner and glue in a dark biscuit and after finishing off when dried the joint looks very nice as well as being much stronger.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That's an awesome suggestion, and I bet that contrast of wood, would look fantastic. I got to try that! Thank you mate! :)

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @MyNameIsLukey no worries, the bigger the difference between wood colours the better it looks

author: @bestowicprimer8835
text: I know your a great maker both yall. hmmm I've been having trouble making a dream happen basically its something I saw on youtube a tesla turbine that uses negative pressure and has a dual stage system a compartment separating basically 2 Tesla turbines first goes through boundary layer and turns as a turbine going through the center then goes through a compartment were it goes from the center and out through the boundary layer acting as a pump. Its been made and all the math has been done it in theory could be made efficient enough to freeze the carbon in the air to dry ice at one extreme.. but the thing is this turbine pump system creates its own vacuum! so you start it and turn your starter vacuum pump off and it will keep running until it fails from vibration or takes all the energy out of the circuit.... i mean throw a black waterhose on your roof or paint a black tank or dig a hole or even better desalinate some ocean water... Thing is all this can be done and everyone can live off grid and produce excess power. but it seems this challenge has not come across the right people to accept the challenge yet... or its taking a very very long time. just figured id rant a little tonight.

author: @TheKamiBunny
text: Or, hear me out, if the appearance of one side doesn't matter, just staple across the joint and bend the staple in the middle slightly to create some pressure. Do 1 staple approximately every inch, roughly.

author: @rayg436
text: You could also use a popsicle stick with a 45 degree cut in the corner.
